title: Quarterly business review narrative — the FleetOps house style
content:
Account: Northgate Distribution (illustrative — not a seeded book account)
Tier: Professional (Live Map, Route Optimizer, Maintenance Radar)

Outcomes achieved this quarter:
- Live Map: 96 of 100 licensed assets reporting daily, up from 71 last quarter.
- Maintenance Radar: 14 predictive alerts issued, 11 acted on before failure, 3 dismissed by the customer's own maintenance team as false positives (flagged to product, not hidden from this narrative).
- Route Optimizer: still not activated — this is the one purchased capability with zero adoption, carried forward from last quarter's plan.

Adoption vs. purchased capability: the account is using 2 of its 3 entitled modules well. Route Optimizer's non-adoption isn't a data or integration problem — the account's dispatch team has not yet been trained on it. That's a training gap, not a product gap, and it's the one place this quarter's plan under-delivered.

Risks, with evidence: reply latency to the CSM's outbound messages has stretched from an average of 6 hours to 22 hours over the trailing 21 days (comms extractor, reply_latency_trend). No case or health-band signal has moved yet. Read alongside the Route Optimizer non-adoption, this looks like early disengagement risk, not yet a churn signal.

Next-quarter plays: (1) schedule Route Optimizer training with the dispatch team, owner FleetOps + Northgate ops lead, before the next QBR; (2) a direct check-in on the latency stretch before it becomes a pattern, separate from the training conversation so one doesn't get lost inside the other.

What this account is not: not a renewal risk today (health remains green, no case backlog), not an expansion candidate this quarter (usage hasn't earned that conversation yet).
